_metadata.description: Controlled vocabulary of 32 excipient/inactive-ingredient functional roles (binder, lubricant, emulsifier, etc.) used as the source of truth for functional_roles[] arrays across harmful_additives.json, other_ingredients.json, and botanical_ingredients.json. Display-only — no scoring impact in V1; ships as a bundled asset to the Flutter app for tap-to-learn UI. Aligned with FDA 21 CFR 170.3(o), EU E-numbers, and FAO/JECFA INS classes. Clinician-locked at v1.0.0; adding/removing roles requires a fresh review cycle.
_metadata.purpose: Controlled vocabulary of excipient/inactive-ingredient functional roles for the Flutter app's tap-to-learn UI. Each role's `name` and `notes` are user-facing copy. `regulatory_references` lets users tap through to the actual FDA CFR / EU E-number text. Single source of truth for `functional_roles[]` IDs across harmful_additives.json, other_ingredients.json, and botanical_ingredients.json.
_metadata.alignment: FDA 21 CFR 170.3(o)(1-32) functional purpose categories + EU E-number functional classes (Annex II of Regulation 1333/2008) + FAO/JECFA INS functional classes

field_contract.id: snake_case stable identifier — used as the value in `functional_roles[]` arrays. Never rename without a migration.
field_contract.name: User-facing chip label (Title Case). Shown directly in the Flutter UI.
field_contract.notes: ≤200 char user-facing description. Plain English, no jargon. Shown in tap modal.
field_contract.regulatory_references: Tappable references — array of {jurisdiction, code}. Flutter renders as 'Learn more' links.
field_contract.examples: Up to 5 common ingredient names users might recognize on labels.

v1_1_followups: Add `attributes` object per ingredient (is_branded_complex, is_synthetic_form, source_origin, caramel_class, e171_eu_concern, is_animal_derived) — separate from functional_roles. See Section 6 of CLINICIAN_REVIEW.md.

Pair each chip's neutral definition with a per-chip deeper-dive screen carrying safety context (FODMAP/IBS for sugar alcohols, EU vs FDA divergence for E171/TiO2, Class III/IV 4-MEI for caramel color, magnesium stearate consumer-advocacy debate).
_metadata.v1_excluded_roles: chelating_agent (folded into preservative/antioxidant); enzyme (folded into processing_aid; active enzymes go to ingredient pipeline); firming_agent (effectively absent in supplements); sequestrant (overlaps preservative/antioxidant); texturizer (synonymous with gelling_agent).

functional_roles.name: Binder
functional_roles.notes: Holds tablet ingredients together so the pill stays intact during handling and shipping.

functional_roles.name: Disintegrant
functional_roles.notes: Helps the tablet break apart in the stomach so the active ingredients can be absorbed.

functional_roles.name: Lubricant
functional_roles.notes: Keeps powder from sticking to the tablet press during manufacturing. Used in trace amounts.

functional_roles.name: Glidant
functional_roles.notes: Improves powder flow during manufacturing so each tablet gets a consistent dose.

functional_roles.name: Coating
functional_roles.notes: Outer film that makes capsules and tablets easier to swallow or protects them from stomach acid.

functional_roles.name: Filler
functional_roles.notes: Adds bulk so a small dose can fit into a normal-sized tablet or capsule.

functional_roles.name: Emulsifier
functional_roles.notes: Lets oil and water mix so the formula stays uniform — common in liquid and gel supplements.

functional_roles.name: Surfactant
functional_roles.notes: Lowers surface tension to help ingredients dissolve and disperse evenly in liquid.

functional_roles.name: Thickener
functional_roles.notes: Increases viscosity — used in syrups, gummies, and gel caps for the right texture.

functional_roles.name: Stabilizer
functional_roles.notes: Keeps the formula from separating, settling, or losing potency over the product's shelf life.

functional_roles.name: Gelling Agent
functional_roles.notes: Forms gels — used in soft-gel capsule shells, gummies, and gel-textured supplements.

functional_roles.name: Humectant
functional_roles.notes: Holds moisture in — keeps soft-gel capsules and gummies from drying out and cracking.

functional_roles.name: Preservative
functional_roles.notes: Helps reduce the growth of bacteria, yeast, and mold so the product stays usable through its shelf life.

functional_roles.name: Antioxidant
functional_roles.notes: Prevents fats and oils in the product from going rancid, preserving freshness and potency.

functional_roles.name: Natural Colorant
functional_roles.notes: Color from a plant, mineral, or animal source. Meets FDA standards without batch-by-batch testing.

functional_roles.name: Artificial Colorant
functional_roles.notes: Synthetic dye that must be FDA-approved. Some users prefer to avoid these.

functional_roles.name: Natural Flavor
functional_roles.notes: Flavor derived from a plant or animal source through standard food-processing methods.

functional_roles.name: Artificial Flavor
functional_roles.notes: Flavor synthesized in a lab. Some users prefer to avoid these.

functional_roles.name: Flavor Enhancer
functional_roles.notes: Strengthens or balances existing flavors — adds little taste of its own.

functional_roles.name: Natural Sweetener
functional_roles.notes: Caloric sweetener from a natural source — provides sweetness and energy.

functional_roles.name: Artificial Sweetener
functional_roles.notes: Zero- or low-calorie synthetic sweetener. Hundreds of times sweeter than sugar by weight.

functional_roles.name: Sugar Alcohol
functional_roles.notes: Lower-calorie sweetener (xylitol, erythritol, sorbitol). Can cause bloating or laxative effects, especially at higher intakes.

functional_roles.name: Anti-Caking Agent
functional_roles.notes: Prevents powders from clumping — keeps capsule contents free-flowing and easy to dose.

functional_roles.name: Anti-Foaming Agent
functional_roles.notes: Reduces foam during manufacturing — used in liquid and powder processing.

functional_roles.name: Processing Aid
functional_roles.notes: Used during manufacturing only — present in the final product at trace levels with no functional effect.

functional_roles.name: Solvent
functional_roles.notes: Liquid used to dissolve or extract other ingredients during manufacturing.

functional_roles.name: Carrier Oil
functional_roles.notes: Edible oil that delivers fat-soluble actives (vitamins A/D/E/K, CoQ10, omega-3s) for absorption.

functional_roles.name: Acidulant
functional_roles.notes: Adds tartness or lowers pH for taste, preservation, or absorption.

functional_roles.name: pH Regulator
functional_roles.notes: Buffers the formula at a target pH — keeps actives stable and palatable.

functional_roles.name: Propellant
functional_roles.notes: Pressurizes spray and aerosol products. Rare in oral supplements; common in throat sprays.

functional_roles.name: Glazing Agent
functional_roles.notes: Coating that gives tablets a shiny finish or seals capsules. Cosmetic and protective only.

functional_roles.name: Prebiotic Fiber
functional_roles.notes: Fiber that selectively feeds beneficial gut bacteria. May cause gas or bloating during the first 1-2 weeks of use as gut bacteria adjust.
